title: CLI: Output generated extension method source code for service types
description:
Enhance the IoCTools CLI to output the generated source code for service collection extension methods.

**Current State:**
- `services-path` outputs the path to the generated registration extension file
- `services` outputs a summary of registrations (lifetimes, interface/implementation pairings)
- Users must navigate to the file to see actual generated code

**Requested Feature:**
Add a command (or flag) to output the actual generated source code for:
1. The full extension method file (all registrations)
2. Registration code for a specific service type (filtered view)

**Use Cases:**
- Quickly inspect what IoCTools generates without finding/opening files
- Copy-paste generated code for documentation or debugging
- CI/scripting integration where raw source output is needed
- Understanding how a specific service gets registered

**Proposed Implementation:**
- New `services-source` command or `--source` flag on `services`
- Optional `--type` filter to show only specific service registrations
- Output to stdout by default for piping/redirection
close_reason: Duplicate of ioc-prefixed beads. Old IoCTools- prefix replaced with ioc- prefix.

title: Stabilize IoCTools analyzers for Delta compatibility
description:
Stabilize IoCTools analyzer rules for Delta project compatibility. Key goals:

**Implicit Lifetime Behavior:**
- No IOC069/070/071 when lifetime is implicit default
- Explicit `[Scoped]` redundant with implicit default → IOC033
- Configurable default implicit lifetime (likely scoped)

**Hosted Services:**
- Pure hosted service → implicit lifetime only; explicit is redundant (IOC072)
- Hosted service + extra interfaces must specify lifetime (IOC070)

**Cross-Assembly Skip:**
- Skip IOC001/IOC002 when dependency implementation lives in another assembly
- Clean architecture: Application shouldn't reference Infrastructure

**Framework Dependencies:**
- ILogger, IConfiguration, options wrappers must NOT trigger IOC001

**Severity Expectations:**
- Missing dep, circular dep → errors
- Manual registration clash → error-level

Outstanding work items documented in prompt.md including Delta build fixes.
close_reason: Duplicate of ioc-prefixed beads. Old IoCTools- prefix replaced with ioc- prefix.

5. **Hosted service redundancy**: Ensure IOC072 covers explicit lifetime on pure hosted; hosted+interfaces needs lifetime (IOC070)
6. **Mediator skip / framework dep skip**: Confirm IOC001 not raised for Mediator handlers or framework abstractions

title: BUG: IOC041 false positive - detects IoCTools generated constructors as manual
description:
ManualConstructorMixingValidator.cs detects IoCTools' OWN generated constructors as 'manual' constructors, causing false positive IOC041 errors.

**Evidence from Delta testing:**
- 98 IOC041 errors reported
- All point to generated .g.cs files (e.g., Delta_Infrastructure_Persistence_MigrationService_Constructor.g.cs:17:5)
- MigrationService.cs has NO manual constructor - only [DependsOn] and [DependsOnConfiguration] attributes

**Root Cause:** Lines 26-30 in ManualConstructorMixingValidator.cs:
```csharp
var manualConstructors = classSymbol.InstanceConstructors
    .Where(ctor => ctor.MethodKind == MethodKind.Constructor)
    .Where(ctor => ctor.DeclaringSyntaxReferences.Length > 0)  // Generated ctors also have syntax!
    .ToList();
```

**Fix Options:**
1. Filter out constructors where DeclaringSyntaxReferences point to .g.cs files
2. Check for [GeneratedCode] attribute on the constructor
3. Track generated symbols internally and exclude them from validation

**Severity:** High - this affects ALL projects using IoCTools constructor generation

**Discovered via:** Manual CLI testing against Delta (ioc-7qi)
close_reason: Fixed IOC041 false positive by excluding .g.cs constructors from validation. All 1244 tests pass.

title: Extract BaseConstructorCallBuilder class
description:
**Location:** ConstructorGenerator.cs:393-514 (121 lines)

**Problem:** Complex nested conditionals with 10+ boolean flags:
- canBaseAcceptDIParameters
- isBaseClassPartial
- baseIsHostedService
- baseHasInjectFields
- baseHasInjectConfigurationFields
- baseHasDependsOnAttribute
- baseHasConditionalServiceAttribute
- baseHasRegisterAsAllAttribute
- baseHasRegisterAsAttribute
- baseClassWillHaveConstructor

**Fix:** Extract to dedicated BaseConstructorCallBuilder:
```csharp
internal class BaseConstructorCallBuilder
{
    public static string Build(INamedTypeSymbol? baseClass, List<ParameterInfo> params)
    {
        var baseInfo = AnalyzeBaseClass(baseClass);
        if (!baseInfo.NeedsConstructorCall) return "";
        return FormatBaseCall(MatchParametersToBase(baseInfo, params));
    }
}
```

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Extract to separate class file
- [ ] Clear decision tree replacing boolean soup
- [ ] All inheritance tests pass

if (line.Contains(typeName, StringComparison.Ordinal) &&

**Fix:** Use Roslyn syntax tree analysis (already available in RegistrationSummaryBuilder) instead of string matching. Extract to RegistrationSummaryBuilder.FilterByType(summary, typeFilter).

- [ ] Add unit tests for edge cases (type name in comments, strings)

title: Add cycle detection for nested configuration objects
description:

{

**Risk:** Runtime binding failure or infinite recursion.

**Fix:** Add cycle detection to ConfigurationValidator.ValidateTypeForConfiguration using HashSet<ITypeSymbol> tracking visited types.

- [ ] Add test for deeply nested (non-circular) configuration

DECISION NEEDED: Should IOC081 severity be Error or Warning?

OPTIONS:
1. Keep as Error - Duplicate registrations are a hard error, users must fix before build succeeds

title: Improve DiagnosticsRunner exception handling
description:
**Location:** DiagnosticsRunner.cs:102-105

**Problem:** Broad catch block swallows all exceptions, only reports generic diagnostic, loses stack trace.

**Current:**
```csharp
catch (Exception ex)
{
    GeneratorDiagnostics.Report(context, "IOC996", "error", ex.Message);
}
```

**Fix:**
```csharp
catch (Exception ex) when (ex is not OutOfMemoryException and not StackOverflowException)
{
    GeneratorDiagnostics.Report(context, "IOC996", 
        "Diagnostic validation pipeline error", 
        $"{ex.GetType().Name}: {ex.Message}\n{ex.StackTrace}");
    throw; // Re-throw to prevent downstream corruption
}
```

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Filter out critical exceptions (OOM, StackOverflow)
- [ ] Include exception type and stack trace in diagnostic
- [ ] Re-throw after reporting
- [ ] Add test forcing exception in validator
close_reason: Completed: Improved exception handling in DiagnosticsRunner with critical exception filtering, detailed error reporting (type + stack trace), and re-throw to prevent corruption. Added test to verify implementation.

title: Extract shared attribute scanning in ServiceDiscovery
description:
**Location:** ServiceDiscovery.cs:60-76

**Problem:** `HasInjectFieldsAcrossPartialClasses` and `HasInjectConfigurationFieldsAcrossPartialClasses` share 90% identical code for syntax-based attribute scanning.

**Duplicated pattern (appears twice):**
```csharp
foreach (var declaringSyntaxRef in classSymbol.DeclaringSyntaxReferences)
    if (declaringSyntaxRef.GetSyntax() is TypeDeclarationSyntax typeDeclaration)
        foreach (var fieldDeclaration in typeDeclaration.DescendantNodes().OfType<FieldDeclarationSyntax>())
            foreach (var attributeList in fieldDeclaration.AttributeLists)
                foreach (var attribute in attributeList.Attributes)
```

**Fix:** Extract to shared method:
```csharp
private static bool HasFieldWithAttributeAcrossPartialClasses(
    INamedTypeSymbol classSymbol, 
    params string[] attributeNames)
```

- [ ] All tests pass unchanged

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
DISCOVERED-FROM: ioc-fqr.6

Current CI workflow uses conditional logic (if: success() && github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main') to control when publish runs. Industry best practice is to have separate workflows:
- ci-pr.yml: Runs on PR, does build+test only
- ci-main.yml: Runs on push to main, does build+test+publish

Benefits:
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- No secrets needed in PR workflow
- Easier to understand and maintain

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] Create .github/workflows/ci-pr.yml for PR validation
- [ ] Create .github/workflows/ci-main.yml for main branch (includes publish)
- [ ] Update/delete main.yml as appropriate

title: CRITICAL: Eliminate 176-line duplication in RegisterAs methods
description:
**Location:** ServiceRegistrationGenerator.RegisterAs.cs:9-206

**Problem:** `GetRegisterAsRegistrations` (lines 9-96) and `GetRegisterAsRegistrationsWithSourceContext` (lines 98-185) are nearly identical, differing only in context type.

**Impact:**
- 176 lines of duplicated logic
- Bug fixes must be applied twice
- Maintenance burden increases with each change

**Fix:** Extract common logic to private helper:
```csharp
private static IEnumerable<ServiceRegistration> GetRegisterAsRegistrationsCore(
    INamedTypeSymbol classSymbol,
    AttributeData registerAsAttribute,
    string lifetime,
    Action<Diagnostic> reportDiagnostic)
{
    // ... shared implementation
    reportDiagnostic(diagnostic); // abstract the reporting
}

// Thin wrappers
internal static IEnumerable<ServiceRegistration> GetRegisterAsRegistrations(...)
    => GetRegisterAsRegistrationsCore(..., context.ReportDiagnostic);
```

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Extract GetRegisterAsRegistrationsCore method
- [ ] Both public methods delegate to core
- [ ] All RegisterAs tests pass unchanged
close_reason: Extracted GetRegisterAsRegistrationsCore method. Both public methods now delegate to core. File reduced from 207 to 133 lines (-74 lines, -36%). All 1261 tests pass.
